subject: English
skill: Advanced Word Analysis and Multisyllable Decoding

lesson.story_mission: Enter the Mountain Word Lab, where every long word opens a trail marker when you read it by syllables and meaningful parts.
lesson.objective: I can decode multisyllable words using syllables, prefixes, suffixes, roots, and vowel patterns.
lesson.concept_explanation: Advanced readers do not guess at long words. They look for syllable breaks, notice vowel patterns, find prefixes and suffixes, identify roots, read each part, blend the word, and check that the meaning fits.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may split a syllable in the wrong place, confuse a prefix with a suffix, forget a root meaning, or guess a multisyllable word from only the first letters.
lesson.real_world_connection: These strategies help you read science terms, social studies words, directions, novels, and new vocabulary independently.
lesson.lesson_summary: Find meaningful parts, mark syllables and vowel patterns, read the parts, blend the whole word, and check the meaning.
lesson.mini_lesson: First scan for prefixes and suffixes. Next find roots and syllables. Then read each chunk aloud and blend the word smoothly.

audio.text: syllable, prefix, suffix, root, morpheme, multisyllable
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Watch: remote splits re | mote. The first syllable is open, and the final e helps the long o sound in mote.

audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Demo: preview has pre- plus view. The prefix pre- means before, so preview means view before.

audio.text: preview. pre means before. view means look.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Practice: portable has the root port, which means carry, plus -able, which means able to.

audio.text: portable. port means carry. able means able to.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

worked_examples.text: Challenge: unbelievable is built from un, believe, and able. Read the parts, then blend: unbelievable.

audio.text: un plus believe plus able makes unbelievable.
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the word or word parts with me.

hint_ladder: Do not guess from the first few letters.
Look for prefixes, suffixes, roots, and vowel patterns.
Read each part, blend the word, then check the meaning in the sentence.

guided_practice.prompt: Which syllable break helps you read remote?
hints: Do not guess from the first few letters.
Look for prefixes, suffixes, roots, and vowel patterns.
Read each part, blend the word, then check the meaning in the sentence.
guided_practice.explanation: re-mote is correct because careful readers use the word parts before blending the whole word.
guided_practice.feedback: Nice decoding. You used the parts to read and understand the word.

misconception_bank.syllable_division_error: The student splits syllables in a way that hides the vowel pattern or creates unreadable chunks.
misconception_bank.prefix_suffix_confusion: The student identifies an affix as the wrong kind of word part or uses the wrong affix meaning.
misconception_bank.root_meaning_confusion: The student reads the word but misses how the root contributes to meaning.
misconception_bank.multisyllable_guessing: The student guesses the whole word before reading all syllables and morphemes.

game_theme.title: Mountain Word Lab
game_theme.name: Mountain Word Lab
game_theme.mission_intro: Decode mountain trail words by splitting syllables, reading word parts, and blending carefully.
game_theme.completion_message: You decoded long words like a Grade 4 word scientist.

story.text: Listen to this page. In this mission, you will climb the Mountain Word Lab by reading long words in smart chunks. Instead of guessing from the first letters, you will look for syllables, prefixes, suffixes, roots, and vowel patterns. Press Start Mission when you are ready to become a word analysis trailblazer.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches advanced word analysis and multisyllable decoding. A long word gets easier when you find meaningful parts, read each syllable, blend the parts, and check the meaning. Watch for common traps like splitting a syllable in the wrong spot or mixing up a prefix and suffix. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page models the strategy with a real word. Step one, find useful word parts and vowel patterns. Step two, read each syllable or morpheme slowly. Step three, blend the whole word and ask whether the meaning fits. Press Next to see another guided example.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how syllables and morphemes work together. Notice how a prefix can come before a base word, a suffix can come after it, and a root can carry the main meaning. Say the parts aloud before you blend the word. Press Next when you are ready to practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Read the word parts and use the visual model before answering. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud, and press Listen when you want pronunciation support for a word or part. Choose or write your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. These words may have several syllables or more than one meaningful part, so slow down and check every chunk. Press Read Question for the question narration, and use Listen to hear multisyllable words and word parts. Answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show that you can decode multisyllable words with syllables, affixes, roots, and vowel patterns. Press Read Question if you want help hearing the prompt, and use Listen for pronunciation support. Read each part, blend the word, and choose or write your answer.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your careful decoding. You earned credit for splitting words clearly, using word meanings, and blending multisyllable words instead of guessing. Remember this strategy whenever a new long word appears. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth as a Grade 4 word analyst. Review your accuracy, hints, and next steps to decide what to practice. If one level needs more work, choose Practice This Skill and return to that level from the Skill Practice Center.
profile.label: Read This Page
